title: digitaria pruriens var. arnottiana
intro: Digitaria pruriens var. arnottiana, also known as Dhamasa in Sanskrit, is a type of grass found in tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in India and Southeast Asia, and is classified as a type of grass under the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to contribute to its various health benefits. The health benefits of Digitaria pruriens var. arnottiana include its potential to help manage fever, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, as well as its traditional use in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, and powders, and is used in various forms, including infusions, poultices, and topical applications.
